lines: A brick sits a finger proud of the rest, low in the old wall where the cold comes through. Dust is missing from its edges. It rocks when you lean on it.

You press it home. Something behind the stone gives, and a slab grinds back off a doorway dressed to look like wall. Fresh scrapes show along the groove. Cut steps go down past the reach of the light, and the smell that climbs them is dry bone, old dust, and lamp smoke.
No plan of the house marks this doorway. The warden hid it first. Someone else has worked the brick since.

effects.log: You take the cut steps down into the cold.
You set the brick back and leave the doorway closed.
